name: Driftwood
text: The sea's last deliveries, still washing loose from the flats.

name: Wreck-Timber
text: Hull planking. Someone sailed this. Build gently.

name: Salt-Stiff Rope
text: Rigging that outlived its ship.

name: Salt
text: The one thing there is no shortage of.

name: Bronze Salvage
text: Fittings, nails, a ship's bell melted small.

name: Ship-Cloth
text: Sailcloth, faded to the color of the sky it worked under.

name: Coralwood
text: Timber from a forest where every tree is a dead animal.

name: Reef-Iron
text: Ore seeded through petrified coral. The Reefkin call it 'marrow' and would rather you didn't.

name: Pearl
text: The reef's last soft thing.

name: Tidebrass
text: Metal from the first-age wrecks. It remembers being wet.

name: Storm-Glass
text: Sand fused by Maren's own hand. The priests trade sermons for it.

name: Trench Pearl
text: Grown in the dark by something that did not mean to make anything beautiful.

name: God-Water
text: Brinefolk-distilled. The best worship offering there is — mind how you came by it.

name: Crab Meat
text: Sweet, raw, and honest. Old Ghal is watching how you phrase that.

name: Smoked Crab
text: Salt and smoke and patience — a brinewife's answer to a hard day. Curing it fed Halor a little, too.

name: Dust-Eel Meat
text: Chewy, quick energy. Tastes like the flats smell.

name: Smoked Eel
text: The reef-runners swear by it: a coil of it in the pack and the horizon gets closer.

name: Remnant of the Shell
text: A divine fragment — the endurance of the old sea, shelled in something you can hold. Enshrine it at a chapel, or consume it, and hear a warm voice approve.

name: Verse of the Harpoon-Song
text: A fragment of an old whaler's song about a harpoon the Storm-Mother threw and lost. Three verses, they say, and the song teaches the forging. This one is yours now.

name: Maren's Own Harpoon

legend.history: She threw it once, at something that deserved it, and the storm has been looking for it ever since. Strike true and the bolt comes down on your mark.

text: A legend of the storm-line.

name: Neris's Hourglass

legend.history: Filled with the last wet sand in the world. Turn it and, once a day, ten seconds agree to happen again.

text: A legend of the tide-line.

name: The Salt-Father's Hearthstone

legend.history: A village heart. Set it in your hall and nothing within the walls spoils — food, stores, or resolve.

text: A legend of the hearth-line.

name: The Unlit Crown

legend.history: It costs what you'd expect. It gives more than you'd hope. That is how he works.

text: A legend of the dark-line.

name: The Lighthouse-Keeper's Lantern

legend.history: The keeper lit it the night the sea left, and swore it would burn until the water came back for it. He kept half the promise. The dark biomes respect it.

text: It never goes out.

name: Driftwood Club
text: The sea's first gift to anyone in trouble: a heavy piece of somewhere else's boat.

name: Bronze Knife
text: Salvage-bronze, workbench-ground. It held a ship together; it will hold you together.

name: Salt Votive
text: A small pressed cone of clean salt. The gods accept what the world has plenty of, if it is given with care.

name: Salt Ration
text: Cured, wrapped, dependable. The taste of getting home anyway.

name: Salt-Crust Cloak
text: Ship-cloth stiffened with salt until it turns a claw. Smells like the flats. Everything does.

name: Bronze-Scale Vest
text: Salvage-bronze sewn to a cloth backing, scale over scale. Heavy, honest, and it has stopped worse than a hound.
